candidate_paths.name: Send email via SMTP
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Alternative sending method to the HTTP API, requires different credentials and setup.
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Mailgun supports SMTP as an alternative to the HTTP API for sending email, useful for legacy systems or applications preferring SMTP.

steps.action: Sign up for a Mailgun account
details: Visit Mailgun signup page
Complete registration form
Confirm email if required
steps.failure_or_wait: Registration errors or email confirmation pending

steps.action: Add and verify a sending domain
details: Add domain in Mailgun control panel
Receive DNS records (SPF, DKIM) to add to domain DNS provider
Wait for DNS propagation and verification
steps.input: Domain name and DNS provider access

details: Access SMTP credentials section
Copy SMTP username and password
steps.failure_or_wait: Access denied or credentials not generated

steps.action: Send a test email via SMTP
details: Configure SMTP client with Mailgun SMTP server and credentials
Compose email with sender, recipient, subject, and body
Send email through SMTP
steps.failure_or_wait: SMTP error response or connection failure
steps.input: SMTP client configuration and email content
steps.success_signal: SMTP 250 OK response indicating email accepted

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Email sent and accepted by Mailgun API
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: HTTP API response indicates success and email is accepted for delivery
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully send an email using the Mailgun HTTP API
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The quickstart guide defines sending your first email via the API as the initial success milestone for developers integrating Mailgun.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has an email sending use case
Developer has access to a domain or sandbox domain for testing
entry_point.developer_intent: Send an email using Mailgun's API

friction_gates.description: Domain ownership verification via DNS records (SPF and DKIM) setup is required to send email from that domain.
API key or SMTP credentials are required to authenticate and authorize sending email.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Mailgun account to access the platform and API keys
Add and verify a domain you own by setting up DNS records (SPF and DKIM) or use the sandbox domain for testing
Obtain API key or SMTP credentials to authenticate API requests

primary_path.action: Sign up for a Mailgun account
details: Visit Mailgun signup page
Complete registration form
Confirm email if required
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Registration errors or email confirmation pending

primary_path.action: Add and verify a sending domain
details: Add domain in Mailgun control panel
Receive DNS records (SPF, DKIM) to add to domain DNS provider
Wait for DNS propagation and verification
primary_path.input: Domain name and DNS provider access

details: Access API credentials section
Copy private API key for use in requests
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Access denied or key not generated

primary_path.action: Send a test email via Mailgun HTTP API
details: Construct HTTP POST request to Mailgun send endpoint
Include API key authentication
Specify sender, recipient, subject, and message body
primary_path.failure_or_wait: HTTP error response or validation error
primary_path.input: HTTP API request with email parameters and API key
primary_path.output: HTTP response indicating success or failure
primary_path.success_signal: HTTP 200 OK with success message and accepted email

alternatives_considered.name: SMTP
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SMTP is supported as a legacy and flexible option but the HTTP API is preferred for programmatic scaling and ease of use.

surface.name: HTTP API
surface.selection_basis: The official quickstart and developer documentation emphasize sending email via the HTTP API as the primary modern integration method, highlighting its ease of use and REST interface.

uncertainties.impact: Time to first success is unknown, limiting comparison to other platforms on onboarding speed.
uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending first email) claimed or expected by Mailgun?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit time to first success or onboarding duration is stated in the official documentation or quickstart guides.
